overview: Add a new top-level `ns` command group with an initial `list` subcommand that lists namespaces in the current Kubernetes context, marks the current namespace with the same format as `ctx list`, respects `--kubeconfig`, follows existing Cobra command conventions, and updates tests and documentation accordingly.

userStories.title: Add namespace command group
userStories.description: Introduce a new top-level `ns` Cobra command and register it with the root command using the same structural pattern already used for `ctx`, so future namespace-related subcommands have a consistent home.
acceptanceCriteria: A new top-level `viewnode ns` command exists and appears in the root command help output.
The `ns` command is defined in a dedicated command package that mirrors the existing `ctx` command organization.
The command help text describes `ns` as the root for namespace-related operations without changing existing root command behavior.
userStories.notes: Reuse existing Cobra patterns from `cmd/ctx` and avoid introducing a parallel command structure outside the established command tree.

userStories.title: Implement namespace list output
userStories.description: Add `viewnode ns list` to load namespaces from the current cluster context, sort them deterministically, and print them with `[*]` for the current namespace and `[ ]` for all others, matching `ctx list` output style.
acceptanceCriteria: Running `viewnode ns list` prints one line per discovered namespace in deterministic order.
The namespace returned by the existing configuration setup as current is marked with `[*]` and all other namespaces are marked with `[ ]`.
The command respects the existing global `--kubeconfig` flag when resolving the client configuration and current namespace.
Failures during namespace discovery are returned through the command error path instead of being silently ignored.
userStories.notes: Use cluster API state for namespace enumeration, not kubeconfig parsing. Keep output and wording aligned with `cmd/ctx/list.go`.

userStories.title: Cover namespace listing with tests
userStories.description: Add automated coverage for the new namespace command so the listing behavior, current marker behavior, kubeconfig-aware setup, and at least one error path are verified in the repository's existing Go test style.
acceptanceCriteria: Automated tests verify successful `ns list` output for multiple namespaces including the current marker.
Automated tests verify at least one namespace discovery failure case.
The new tests follow existing command test patterns and pass with `make test`.
userStories.notes: Follow the style of `cmd/ctx/ctx_test.go`, but use suitable API mocking or fake clients because namespace lists come from the cluster rather than kubeconfig content alone.

userStories.title: Document the namespace list command
userStories.description: Update user-facing command documentation so the new `ns` command group and `ns list` subcommand are reflected anywhere command listings or usage examples are maintained manually.
acceptanceCriteria: The command reference documents the new `ns` command group and `ns list` subcommand.
Any added example or wording matches the existing CLI terminology used in the repository.
userStories.notes: Keep documentation changes limited to the new namespace command and do not expand scope into future namespace operations.
